title: Budget Reconciliation
instruction: Three department heads sent you their Q1 budget numbers. Jordan Nguyen sent the Engineering figures, Maya Nguyen sent the Marketing figures, and Miles Chen sent the Operations figures. A fourth email from Jordan Kim attempts to summarize all three — but two of the numbers are wrong. Read all four emails, identify the errors, and reply to Jordan Kim's email with the two corrections. Reply only to Jordan Kim (do not Reply All — the summary is CC'd to the CFO and board). Star the three department emails for reference. Create a 'Budget Verified' label and apply it to all four budget emails.

emails.subject: Notes from the partner sync
emails.body:
I pulled the loose ends into one note for easier review.

The team is aligned on the main path and only needs confirmation on the follow-up items. Happy to consolidate comments if more feedback comes in.

Thanks,
Miles

emails.subject: Q1 Budget Summary — please verify
emails.body:
I compiled the Q1 figures from all three departments. Please review:

- Engineering: $142,500
- Marketing: $98,000
- Operations: $205,750

Let me know if anything looks off before I send the final version to the board.

Thanks,
Jordan

emails.subject: Q1 Operations budget figures
emails.body:
Q1 Operations numbers below.

Total: $215,750. Covers facilities, logistics, and vendor contracts.

Thanks,
Miles

emails.subject: Q1 Marketing budget figures
emails.body:
Attached are the Q1 Marketing figures as requested.

Our total for the quarter: $89,000. Includes campaign spend, events, and agency fees.

Thanks,
Maya

emails.subject: Q1 Engineering budget figures
emails.body:
Here are the final Q1 numbers for Engineering.

Total spend: $142,500. This covers salaries, tooling, and infrastructure.

Thanks,
Jordan

emails.subject: Updated rollout checklist
emails.body:
Passing along the current draft so you have it in one place.

Nothing looks blocked right now, but we still need one decision on timing. No response needed unless you want anything reordered.

Thanks,
Sofia

emails.subject: Follow-up on venue estimate
emails.body:
I pulled the loose ends into one note for easier review.

The open items are mostly ownership questions rather than scope changes. Let me know if you want the spreadsheet version as well.

Thanks,
Nina
